data.title: Ecolab close to deal for KKR's data-center cooling company - WSJ
data.description:
Ecolab (ECL) is close to a deal to purchase data-center cooling company CoolIT Systems from KKR (KKR) for between $4.5 billion and $5 billion. Shares of Ecolab fell 2.6%.

A transaction could be announced as soon as next week, according to a  WSJ report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

The price tag is a major increase from CoolIT's ~$270 million valuation when KKR acquired a majority stake in 2023.  Abu Dhabi's sovereign-wealth investor Mubadala owns a minority stake in the cooling business.

data.title: Gold slides 5% and breaks key 100-day MA as safe-haven demand fades
data.description:
Gold (XAUUSD:CUR) and precious metals declined sharply Thursday, with spot gold falling 5.2% to an intraday low near $4,502.39 per ounce, marking its weakest level since mid-January.

The selloff also pushed prices below the key 100-day moving average, reinforcing bearish technical momentum and signaling continued downside pressure in the near term. The retreat reflects a broader shift in investor sentiment, as market participants rotate away from traditional safe-haven assets amid evolving macroeconomic expectations. 

Despite the downturn in underlying commodity prices, several large-cap names in the gold and precious metals sector continue to exhibit relative strength. 

As a result, Seeking Alpha highlighted 15 companies with market capitalizations above $10B that maintain the highest Quant Ratings in the space, underscoring selective resilience even as the broader sector faces sustained pressure. 

  1. Coeur Mining (CDE), quant rating of 4.94.
  2. Impala Platinum Holdings (IMPUY), quant rating of 4.92.
  3. IAMGOLD Corporation (IAG), quant rating of 4.86.
  4. Gold Fields Limited (GFI), quant rating of 4.82.
  5. Newmont Corporation (NEM), quant rating of 4.79.
  6. Barrick Mining Corporation (B), quant rating of 4.77.
  7. AngloGold Ashanti (AU), quant rating of 4.73.
  8. Valterra Platinum (AGPPF), quant rating of 3.43.
  9. Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM), quant rating of 3.42.
  10. Agnico Eagle Mines Limited (AEM), quant rating of 3.42.
  11. Kinross Gold Corporation (KGC), quant rating of 3.41.
  12. Franco-Nevada Corporation (FNV), quant rating of 3.39.
  13. Pan American Silver (PAAS), quant rating of 3.25.
  14. Royal Gold (RGLD), quant rating of 3.24.
  15. Equinox Gold (EQX), quant rating of 3.23. 

Gold and Gold Mining ETFs: (GLD), (IAU), (SGOL), (OUNZ), (BAR), (GDX), (GDXJ), (NUGT), (RING), and (DUST).

data.title: Alaska oil and gas lease sale draws record $163M in bids
data.description:
The U.S. government held its most successful sale ever of oil and gas drilling rights in Alaska's National Petroleum Reserve on Wednesday, attracting more than $163M in winning bids in the first lease sale in the NPR-Alaska in seven years.

Eleven companies submitted bids on 187 tracts of the reserve totaling ~1.3M acres, according to the Interior Department's Bureau of Land Management, which had offered more than 5.5M acres for lease.

A joint venture between Repsol (REPYF) (REPYY) and Shell (SHEL) was the auction's top spender, bidding a total of nearly $94M for 42 tracts.

Other significant winners included ConocoPhillips (COP), which bid on lease tracts adjacent to its existing Willow project, as well as Exxon Mobil (XOM) and North Slope Exploration LLC.

Under the One Big Beautiful Bill Act passed last year, the BLM must hold at least five lease sales in the reserve by 2035, each offering no fewer than 4M acres.

data.title: BP shares uptick for seven consecutive sessions
data.description:
BP (BP) shares clocked seven straight sessions of gains, as the stock rose 3.6% at $46.24 on Thursday.

The energy giant gained over 7% in the preceding six sessions. The stock has surged 34% so far this year, compared to a 3% drop in the broader S&P 500 Index.

BP is up 21% over the past one month.

"BP's strategic pivot away from net zero targets towards core oil and gas growth underpins a bullish investment thesis," pointed out a recent Seeking Alpha analysis. 

Earlier in the day, BP agreed to sell its Gelsenkirchen refinery in Germany to Klesch Group for an undisclosed sum and raised its cost reduction target, as the company continues to simplify its portfolio and shore up its balance sheet.

Looking at Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating, BP has a Hold rating with a score of 3.4 out of 5. The company received A+ in the prospect of profitability, while it received D+ in growth factor.

Turning to the Wall Street community, six analyst gave BP a Buy and above rating. Nine analysts have given the stock a Hold recommendation, while four recommended Sell or lower.

 

Seeking Alpha analysts are cautious and see the stock as a Hold.

data.title: Fmr. Minneapolis Fed president: ‘The Fed was wise to take another stand pat approach’
data.description:
Former Minneapolis Fed president Gary Stern believes the Federal Reserve was “appropriately cautious” at its recent two-day policy meeting, endorsing the central bank’s decision to pause interest rate cuts for the second consecutive time amid growing uncertainty from the war in the Middle East and a complicated domestic economic picture.

Fed funds futures now imply that interest rates will likely remain in their current range through the end of the year.

In an interview with CNBC, Stern emphasized that “uncertainty reigns supreme at the moment,” pointing to the unclear duration of the Middle East conflict and its potential economic effects.

“The Fed was wise to take another stand pat approach and not to claim that they know the path forward at this point,” he said, stressing the importance of the central bank not pretending to know more than it does.

Stern outlined a range of domestic economic headwinds complicating the Fed’s decision-making, including labor market stagnation even as unemployment remains low. He also cited growing concerns about private credit and the “uncertain fate of tariffs” as additional sources of uncertainty facing policymakers.

The inflation picture remains particularly challenging, according to Stern, with energy prices from the Middle East conflict poised to push up both headline and core inflation.

He noted the “persistent stubbornness of inflation above the Fed’s target” even before accounting for the impact of higher energy prices, creating a difficult situation for a central bank tasked with maintaining both high employment and a 2% inflation target.

Regarding international monetary policy, Stern said he would be surprised if foreign central banks took dramatic action in the near term and that the Fed would be unlikely to react significantly if the Bank of England or other central banks moved rates.

He characterized the situation abroad as presenting “a different policy challenge,” given that inflation has been running lower in England while economic growth has lagged the U.S.

Stern concluded that with “a huge amount of uncertainty swirling around,” the Fed’s refusal to project certainty where none exists represents a prudent approach to monetary policy in the current environment.

More on interest rates

  • ECB keeps key interest rates unchanged, committed to 2% inflation target
  • Fed’s no change to interest rates feeds into an elevated sense of uncertainty – economist
  • Wall Street snaps winning streak as Fed keeps rates unchanged, inflation fears loom
  • Federal Reserve blew it by standing pat on rates - economist
  • America is now $39T in debt. Is there an end in sight?

data.title: Tat Technologies outlines M&A strategy and targets margin expansion through 2026, supported by $550M backlog
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: TAT Technologies Ltd. (TATT) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Igal Zamir reported record revenue, profitability, and long-term agreement growth in 2025, despite "industry challenges, including tariffs and ongoing supply chain constraints across the aviation ecosystem." He highlighted the transition to a widely held public company and the expansion of U.S. institutional investors as an "important corporate milestone." Zamir stated, "As we enter 2026, we do so from a position of strength with the right team, the right capability, strong financial position and expanding high-quality backlog."
  • Zamir explained that the value of long-term agreements and backlog reached approximately $550 million, up from $520 million at the end of Q3 and $429 million at the end of 2024.
  • He detailed segment performance: "In our APU business, we delivered a strong year of growth... increased our market share in the 500 and the 200 APU categories." The heat exchangers segment "continued to generate consistent recurring demand," and landing gear "continues to show growth as the aviation industry enters major MRO maintenance cycle."
  • Zamir noted ongoing supply chain challenges, particularly in APUs and landing gear, but emphasized continued demand and operational agility.
  • On strategic outlook, Zamir said, "M&A is also a clear strategic priority for us in 2026. Our balance sheet and cash position provide the financial capacity to act."
  • CFO Ehud Ben-Yair stated, "Fourth quarter revenue increased by 13% to $46.5 million, up from $41.1 million in the same period last year... For the full year, revenue grew more than 17%."
  • Ben-Yair added, "Gross profit for the quarter increased by 23.6% and gross margin expanded by 210 basis points to the level of 25.2%."

Outlook

  • Zamir expressed optimism for 2026, supported by new long-term agreements and a record backlog. He stated, "Based on the increased backlog and the intake levels we are seeing over the past 3 months, we remain extremely confident in our overall trajectory for the business."
  • Ben-Yair noted, "We are entering 2026 with a record level of backlog, long-term agreement and a very strong balance sheet. These factors should support the growth that we are expecting for this year and support our strategic priorities."
  • Guidance language remains focused on continued revenue growth and margin expansion, driven by operational improvements and M&A.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q4 2025 was $46.5 million, an increase of 13% year-over-year. Full-year revenue grew more than 17%.
  • Gross margin reached 25.2% for Q4, a 210 basis point improvement.
  • Operating income for the quarter was $4.9 million, up 20.2% year-over-year; full-year operating income was $18.8 million.
  • Net income for the quarter was $4.7 million, and $16.8 million for the full year.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q4 was $6.9 million (14.8% margin); for the year, $25.5 million (14.3% margin).
  • Operational cash flow for the quarter was $5.6 million, and $15 million for the year.
  • Cash position at year-end was $51.6 million, with total debt at $11.7 million.

Q&A

  • Benjamin Klieve, Benchmark: Asked if the backlog increase was due to deferred revenue or new contracts. Zamir responded, "the vast majority of the increase that we see in Q4 comes from a new contract that was signed, long-term agreements."
  • Klieve followed up on turnaround time and supply chain impact. Zamir said, "In the last quarter of last year, it completely reversed. And all of a sudden, we are facing challenges again, especially in APUs and landing gear, dramatically extended lead times..."
  • Jonathan Siegmann, Stifel: Questioned impact of higher oil prices and conflict in the Middle East. Zamir replied, "we don't see any impact on the opposite. We see a very strong intake of work coming through our shops across all business lines."
  • Joshua Sullivan, JonesTrading: Asked about APU bid environment versus last year. Zamir stated, "it's a steady state. It's a steady flow of new RFPs that are opening with opportunities."
  • Sullivan also asked about margin potential post-supply constraints. Zamir said, "we believe that we can be... best-in-class... EBITDA is in the range of 20%, a little bit more. That's where we aspire to be."
  • Sergey Glinyanov, Freedom Broker: Asked about defense segment demand. Zamir noted, "defense is a small portion of TAT overall revenue... we definitely see a substantial increase, both on the MRO side... and on the new orders that we are receiving."
  • Glinyanov inquired about M&A focus. Zamir explained, "we are in active process looking for opportunities and for deals... wanting to make sure that we will add value and the deals that we write for TAT."
  • Michael Ciarmoli, Truist: Asked about growth expectations for the 131 APU. Zamir confirmed, "we are expecting to see growth from the 131... we made a huge stride forward last year with contracts, with long-term agreements, with engines..."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed on supply chain risks, backlog quality, margin upside, and M&A execution, with a generally inquisitive, neutral-to-slightly positive tone.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone throughout, especially in prepared remarks. Zamir consistently used language such as "we remain extremely confident" and "we are very pleased with the results."
  • In comparison to the previous quarter, management's tone remains upbeat, with greater emphasis on operational improvements and proactive M&A strategy.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remains focused on growth and margin expansion, but with increased emphasis on M&A as a strategic priority for 2026.
  • Analysts’ questions shifted more toward backlog quality, margin potential, and M&A execution, compared to previous quarter’s focus on operational flexibility and segment opportunities.
  • Gross margin, net income, and cash flow improved sequentially, with the backlog increasing from $520 million in Q3 to $550 million in Q4.
  • Management’s confidence in overcoming supply chain challenges appears resolute, yet more attention is given to M&A opportunity pipeline and scalability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Ongoing supply chain disruptions, especially in APUs and landing gear, are cited as the primary operational risk. Zamir noted, "we are facing challenges again, especially in APUs and landing gear, dramatically extended lead times."
  • Management is actively working with suppliers and customers to address delays but does "not yet see a broad recovery in overall supply chain performance."
  • Seasonal variability in MRO intake and revenue recognition is acknowledged as a continuing factor.

Final Takeaway

TAT Technologies delivered record financial and operational results in 2025, highlighted by double-digit growth, margin expansion, and a robust $550 million backlog. Management emphasized the company's readiness for further growth in 2026, supported by a strengthened balance sheet and a clear focus on accretive M&A opportunities. Despite persistent supply chain headwinds, TAT remains confident in its ability to drive revenue growth and pursue margin expansion, leveraging its diversified aviation platform and proactive operational strategies.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Envela soars after reporting strong bullion and pre-owned jewelry demand
data.description:
Envela (ELA) soared in Thursday trading after the company topped analysts estimates with its fourth-quarter earnings report. The recommerce specialist pointed to strong consumer demand, with bullion and pre-owned jewelry performing particularly well.

Revenue was up 67% year over year to $80.5M, while adjusted EBITDA more than tripled from last year's mark to $8.0M. "Consumer results were driven by favorable bullion market conditions and strong holiday sales, while commercial growth benefited from our IT Asset Disposition platform and solid outbound shipments," highlighted CFO John DeLuca. "These results strengthened our balance sheet, with net cash increasing year over year, supporting our ability to invest in growth and deliver value to shareholders," he added. 

Shares of ELA were up 42.0% at 12:57 p.m. and carved out a new all-time high of $17.24 earlier in the session. The Seeking Alpha Quant Rating on ELA flipped to Buy in February.

data.title: Exxon says FPSO for fifth Guyana offshore oil and gas project 'almost complete'
data.description:
A new floating production facility for the Exxon Mobil-led (XOM) consortium in Guyana is "almost complete" and expected ‌to soon depart from Singapore, the company's head in Guyana, Alistair Routledge, said Thursday, according to Reuters.

The Errea Wittu floating ⁠production, storage and offloading platform is the ​fifth to be installed by the Exxon-led (XOM) group in Guyana, and will produce, store and ​deliver as much as 250K bbl/day from ​the Uaru offshore project.

Exxon (XOM) had said previously that the FPSO would ⁠arrive in Guyanese waters this year, without further details.

Following ​Uaru, ⁠Exxon (XOM) is on track to start the Whiptail project by the end of 2027, while ⁠trying to ​accelerate the startup of ​the Hammerhead project for 2028, Routledge said.

Separately, a joint venture by Exxon (XOM), Energean, and Helleniq Energy has notified Greece it plans to look for gas in a ​western offshore block, the country's hydrocarbons management ​agency said Thursday, Reuters reported.

After assessing the area's potential, the JV notified ​the agency of its plans to proceed to the second phase, ‌which ⁠includes the first offshore exploration drilling in 40 years, the state-owned agency said.

data.title: Intuitive Machines outlines $900M-$1B 2026 revenue target as Lanteris acquisition drives diversification
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Stephen Altemus described 2025 as a transformational year, highlighting the completion of the company’s second lunar mission, expansion into national security space programs, the acquisition of KinetX Aerospace, and the announced acquisition of Lanteris Space Systems. Altemus stated, "we expect 2026 revenue to approach $1 billion, nearly a 5x increase from 2025," and detailed a diversified revenue mix moving toward 40% commercial, 40% civil space, and 20% national security customers.
  • Altemus emphasized the company’s Moon-first infrastructure strategy and detailed progression in low Earth orbit, geostationary, and lunar domains. He highlighted the launch of EchoStar XXV, upcoming SiriusXM satellite deliveries, and the near-completion of NASA’s lunar Gateway Power and Propulsion Element.
  • Altemus noted the integration of Lanteris brings proven satellite production platforms to new growth markets, stating the company is “focused on taking proven production platforms and applying them to new growth markets as a prime operator.”
  • CFO Peter McGrath stated, "Q4 2025 revenue was $44.8 million, driven primarily by CLPS, OMES and NSNS execution... Q4 gross margin came in strong at $8.5 million, which represents a 19% positive gross margin." McGrath also highlighted the completion of the Lanteris acquisition in January and a $175 million strategic equity investment to advance communications data processing networks.

Outlook

  • McGrath stated, "For 2026, we expect revenue in the range of $900 million to $1 billion, representing a transformational step-up in scale for the company. Importantly, roughly 2/3 of our expected 2026 revenue is already supported by contracted backlog, giving us strong visibility into our outlook."
  • The company is targeting positive adjusted EBITDA for 2026 driven by scale from the Lanteris acquisition, growth in higher-margin service revenue, and operational efficiencies.
  • Management noted that the backlog as of February was $943 million, with additional awards pending that could further increase visibility.

Financial Results

  • Q4 2025 revenue was $44.8 million, primarily from CLPS, OMES, and NSNS programs. Gross margin for the quarter was $8.5 million or 19%.
  • OMES revenue in Q4 was $14.7 million. Operating loss for the quarter was $33.1 million, compared to a loss of $13.4 million in Q4 2024, due mainly to acquisition-related expenses and continued investment in growth.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was negative $19.1 million in Q4. Operating cash used was $7.3 million, with capital expenditures of $15.6 million, resulting in negative free cash flow of $22.9 million. Year-end cash balance was $583 million, with $403 million subsequently used for the Lanteris acquisition.
  • As of the end of February, cash was $272 million following the acquisition and capital raise. Year-end backlog was $213.1 million, and combined company backlog reached $943 million by February end.

Q&A

  • Joshua Sullivan, JonesTrading: Asked about Lanteris integration and customer response. Altemus responded integration is "going very well," with transition activities "well ahead of schedule."
  • Sujeeva De Silva, ROTH Capital: Inquired about national security growth and programs. Altemus discussed recent Space Development Agency Tranche 3 win and national security pipeline, including Golden Dome and orbital transfer vehicle development.
  • De Silva also questioned 2026 revenue linearity and upside. McGrath described revenue as "pretty level throughout the year" and noted potential upside if Artemis missions accelerate.
  • Andres Sheppard-Slinger, Cantor Fitzgerald: Asked about new capabilities from the Lanteris acquisition and LTV program award structure. Altemus described expanded opportunities and imminent LTV decision, noting, "we feel it's imminent."
  • Austin Moeller, Canaccord: Sought details on operational changes at Lanteris. Altemus credited Chris Johnson for streamlining and investing in the 300 series, stating the business is "strong and producing."
  • Xin Yu, Deutsche Bank: Asked about architecture for space data centers. Altemus said, "I think edge computing in space and making decision -- doing decision-making in space is the key to the future of data centers as opposed to replacing terrestrial-based data centers."
  • Jonathan Siegmann, Stifel: Queried on LTV timing amid Artemis restructuring. Altemus indicated independence from Artemis schedule, stating, "we're not tied to the sequence of events for Artemis V."
  • Samantha Stiroh, BofA: Asked about the impact of the Space Superiority executive order. Altemus said the company is "working directly with NASA to look at ways to move efforts forward faster."
  • Griffin Boss, B. Riley: Questioned CLPS 2.0 scale. Altemus expects CLPS 2.0 "to be larger than CLPS 1" and highlighted a push for higher cadence and heavier cargo missions.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on integration execution, revenue growth potential, and national security pipeline, with a generally positive and congratulatory tone, seeking additional clarity on growth levers and program awards.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, frequently using phrases like "very excited," "well ahead of schedule," and "the future is very bright," while addressing program risks and operational execution in a straightforward manner.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analyst sentiment shifted further toward optimism, with management exhibiting higher confidence due to acquisition closure and improved financial visibility, and analysts expressing more interest in near-term catalysts and integration progress.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Current quarter guidance for 2026 revenue ($900 million to $1 billion) was provided, compared to previous quarter’s focus on acquisition rationale and strategic positioning without specific revenue guidance.
  • The revenue mix evolved from a 25% defense/25% civil/50% commercial split to an anticipated 40% commercial/40% civil/20% national security blend, reflecting a more balanced and diversified portfolio.
  • The Lanteris acquisition moved from announcement (Q3) to completion (Q4), with integration and customer reception now in focus.
  • Gross margin improved in Q4 versus Q3, with higher service revenue and cost reductions noted.
  • Management tone became more assertive regarding growth and operational execution, while analysts increased focus on concrete integration outcomes and program wins.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited government budget delays and shutdown as impacting program timing and backlog.
  • Integration challenges for Lanteris were noted, though described as ahead of schedule.
  • Analysts questioned the timing of major program awards, competitive landscape amid Artemis restructuring, and the pace of adoption for new service models.
  • Management identified global competition, bandwidth constraints in deep space communications, and the need for continued investment in infrastructure as ongoing challenges.

Final Takeaway

Intuitive Machines leadership highlighted a year of transformation, marked by major acquisitions and portfolio diversification, projecting revenue of $900 million to $1 billion for 2026 and targeting positive adjusted EBITDA. The completion and integration of Lanteris Space Systems positions the company for expanded opportunities across commercial, civil, and national security markets, with a growing backlog and substantial visibility into future revenue. Management maintains strong confidence in execution, supported by high-margin service growth, robust contract awards, and active pursuit of infrastructure-based recurring revenue programs.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: CRWD, TME, UBER, ARM
data.description:
CrowdStrike (CRWD) and Arm Holdings (ARM) have both been upgraded to Hold as analysts point to resilient growth drivers, including strong enterprise demand for cybersecurity products and persistent royalty revenue from smartphone applications. Meanwhile, Tencent Music (TME) and Uber (UBER) face downgrades amid concerns over reduced reporting transparency and competitive threats from autonomous vehicles.

Upgrades

  • CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD): Upgrade to Hold by The Asian Investor. The analyst cites a strong fourth fiscal quarter with record customer acquisition and robust enterprise product uptake, alongside growing AI product adoption driving annual recurring revenue growth.

“I like that CrowdStrike’s ARR business is soaring and that the SaaS enterprise is growing its retention rates. ARR growth and higher customer retention are the two main reasons why I am upgrading CRWD shares from Sell to Hold.”

  • Arm Holdings (ARM): Upgrade to Hold by Jia Ming Eow. Despite concerns in the smartphone sector, the analyst highlights resilient royalty growth from v9 and CSS architectures, along with potential AI chip development opportunities with Nvidia and MediaTek.

“I think that ARM is positioning itself to capitalize on the next phase of AI innovations, with already existing relationships with the largest AI firms like Nvidia. I doubt that ARM will be written off anytime soon despite major fears surrounding their core segments, which have actually shown no signs of a slowdown just yet.”

Downgrades

  • Tencent Music Entertainment Group (TME): Downgrade Buy to Hold by The Value Pendulum. While the company delivered faster revenue growth and wider gross margins in Q4 2025, the analyst expresses concern over a reporting framework shift that reduces data transparency amid intensifying competition.

“The stock's P/E de-rated from 25.1x on Oct 1, 2025 to 18.2x on the last trading day of the year. But it didn't do any repurchases in 4Q25 to take advantage of the cheaper shares. This sends a clear message to me that its operations could be facing stiff competition for a longer-than-expected time.”

  • Uber Technologies (UBER): Downgrade to Hold by PropNotes. The analyst ends a long-standing bullish view, citing slowing revenue growth, potentially non-recurring margin expansion, and mounting competitive threats from autonomous vehicles.

“I believe the company is running out of road, especially on pricing. In other words, I believe there is a ceiling at which the Uber take rate no longer makes sense. Users will coordinate private transfers with local taxis, and drivers will no longer make enough to cover modest wages, depreciation, gas, and other vehicle expenses.”
